cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure this one out. The question is about Mr. Williams and what he was doing when the policeman stopped him. Let me read through the story again to make sure I get the details right. So, the setup is about different crossroads with different signs. Some require stopping, some to go slow but not stop, and others you don't have to do either if you're on the main road. Mr. Williams is a careful driver who encountered a "SLOW" sign. He slowed down, looked both ways, then went across the main road without stopping. Then a policeman stopped him, saying he didn't stop. Mr. Williams explained that the sign was "SLOW," not "STOP," and he did slow down. The policeman then realized he was in the wrong street. The question is asking what Mr. Williams was doing when he was stopped. The choices are: (A) was driving at a high speed (B) was driving onto the main road (C) was going to stop his car (D) was driving slowly From the story, when he got to the crossroads with the "SLOW" sign, he slowed down and then "drove across without stopping completely." So he was going slowly when
